docs.title: Welcome to Meridian 2.0
docs.text:
Meridian is the ultimate pipeline toolkit for pushing data and visuals seamlessly from Blender directly to the Godot Engine.

Whether you're developing high-end desktop releases, setting up baked XR pipelines, or just managing massive scenes, Meridian automates all the heavy lifting for you.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Check out the most important guides below to get your pipeline up and running immediately:

  • Installation Guide
  • Quick Start

docs.title: Core Features
docs.text:
Our documentation dives deep into every component. Ensure you read up on:

  • Preprocessing: Optimize your meshes and materials effortlessly.
  • Lightmapping Workflows: Break the resolution barriers.
  • Livelinking: Beam your 3D scenes directly into the engine in realtime.

Tip: You can use the search bar at the top of the page to quickly find any specific function or configuration option!

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Follow these steps to install the Meridian addon into both Blender and your Godot project.

docs.title: Blender Setup
docs.text:
1. Download the meridian-addon.zip archive.
  2. In Blender, navigate to Edit > Preferences > Add-ons.
  3. Click Install...
  4. Locate the downloaded .zip and install it.
  5. Check the box next to Pipeline: Meridian 2.0 to enable the addon.

docs.title: Godot Setup
docs.text:
1. Copy the addons/meridian folder into your Godot project's res://addons/ directory.
  2. Navigate to Project > Project Settings > Plugins.
  3. Locate Meridian and check the Enable box.

You're now ready to use livelinking and desktop exports!
